title: Fundamental Concepts of Group Theory
description: A visual breakdown of the core components, axioms, and key concepts in abstract algebra group theory.
relationships: A Set and a Binary Operation form a Group if they satisfy Closure, Associativity, Identity, and Inverse axioms. An Abelian Group is a special type of group where the operation is commutative. Subgroups are subsets of a group that are themselves groups under the same operation. Common examples like Integers (ℤ) and Symmetric Groups (Sₙ) illustrate these concepts.
